transcript.text: Hello, Living in Boulder Vlog. It's the 23rd of January 2019 at 1230 p.m. I never got to sleep last night. I think I got up at about three or four. It was kind of late last night. I think I got more than eight hours of sleep.

Um, and, uh, yeah, and then I just worked solidly until then. Yesterday when I got up, I wanted to fix my Native American Missing Legends page on my website because it was, uh, I think it was a page, one of the oldest pages that I've had.

So it had a lot of stuff in there that I had to fix. And, um, yeah, it was really, it wasn't, wasn't functional. It was in a rough draft stage for a long time. So, yeah, I suddenly realized I really wanted to do that. And then I did it and I did all last night. I just finished it now. Um, there were lots of, um, I had to rebuild lots of pages. I love the links.

But, uh, and then I, um, I fixed, uh, um, uh, the, uh, decline and fall of the Roman Empire. Those pages weren't done. And the menu, the links were broken, but I was just a batch edit. I was listening to Aristotle, all these new books from Aristotle, which is interesting.

It sounds Aristotle talking about soul and spirit sounded just like canned giving the idea that these guys, you know, they, they, um, they copy each other, their ideas, Cicero copied the ideas of Plato. But, um, and then after, so, so yeah, I just finished decline and fall. I was adding the numbers to the top part of the menu. Um, yeah, so I just finished that. And then, uh, there's Pelagius. That's another author that I never put up, but it's just one small. It's only a few pages worth.

Because before computers were so slow, you know, that by the time it's done thinking, you forget what you're doing, you know? And so when it goes fast, it's a lot easier.
So, um, today I'm going to go over to my mom's house and help her with something. She's going to call me at three.
